overview: A URL shortener service with a single backend API that generates unique short codes for long URLs and handles redirection. The system uses an in-memory store for simplicity, exposing RESTful endpoints for URL shortening and redirection. Communication is synchronous HTTP with the API serving both developer integration and end-user redirection traffic.
requirements: The API must accept a long URL via POST request and return a unique short code
The API must redirect users from short code to original long URL via GET request
Short codes must be unique across all stored URLs
The same long URL can be shortened multiple times, generating different short codes each time
Short codes must use alphanumeric characters (letters and numbers) only
Short codes must be compact in length
The system must handle collision detection to ensure uniqueness
The API must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks
The redirect must use appropriate HTTP status code (301 or 302)

components.name: url-shortener-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: URL Shortener API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for creating short URLs and redirecting to original URLs
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /shorten:
    post:
      summary: Create a short URL
      operationId: shortenURL
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - url
              properties:
                url:
                  type: string
                  format: uri
                  description: The long URL to shorten
                  example: https://example.com/very/long/path/to/resource
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Short URL created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  shortCode:
                    type: string
                    description: The generated short code
                    example: abc123
                  shortUrl:
                    type: string
                    format: uri
                    description: The complete short URL
                    example: http://localhost:9090/abc123
                  originalUrl:
                    type: string
                    format: uri
                    description: The original long URL
                    example: https://example.com/very/long/path/to/resource
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: url field is required
        '500':
          description: Server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: failed to generate short code
  /{shortCode}:
    get:
      summary: Redirect to original URL
      operationId: redirectToOriginal
      parameters:
        - name: shortCode
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: The short code to redirect
          example: abc123
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to original URL
          headers:
            Location:
              schema:
                type: string
                format: uri
              description: The original long URL
        '404':
          description: Short code not found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: short code not found
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a URL shortener service in Go using net/http on port 9090. Create three endpoints: GET /health returns {"status":"ok"} with 200 status. POST /shorten accepts JSON body with 'url' field containing the long URL to shorten. Generate a unique short code using alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) with length 6-8 characters. Use a random generation approach and check for collisions against existing codes in storage. Store the mapping from short code to original URL in an in-memory map protected by a mutex for thread safety. Return 201 with JSON containing shortCode, shortUrl (full URL with short code), and originalUrl. Return 400 if url field is missing or invalid. Return 500 if code generation fails after reasonable retries. GET /{shortCode} looks up the short code in storage and returns 302 redirect with Location header set to the original URL. Return 404 with JSON error if short code doesn't exist. The same long URL shortened multiple times must generate different short codes each time (no deduplication). Use crypto/rand for generating random codes to ensure uniqueness. Implement proper error handling, request validation, and logging. Use standard library encoding/json for JSON handling. The service should be stateless except for the in-memory URL store. Include Dockerfile with multi-stage build using golang:1.21-alpine for build and alpine:latest for runtime. Expose port 9090. No external dependencies or databases required.
